title: F-003 doctor 换皮 (段落头 + tree + grid + badge) + NO_COLOR snapshot
description: Reskin the fabric doctor human-readable output (packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts) from flat coloured lines to the W3-B structural language per mockups.md#1: section-bar headers (▌ Store Health / ▌ Checks), a tree (├─/└─) for store-health rows, padEnd grid alignment for the store/check rows, and status/scope badges. Touch ONLY the human renderer paths (renderHumanReport / renderStoreDiagnostics / renderTldrHeader / writeIssueSection-headers) — the JSON output path (args.json) MUST stay byte-unchanged. Extract the reskinned section renderers into pure renderX(report):string helpers and ship a NO_COLOR snapshot (C-008).
action: In doctor.ts, replace flat lines with structural primitives: (1) renderStoreDiagnostics' `paint.ai("store health")` → sectionBar("Store Health") followed by tree() rows where each row uses scopeBadge + padEnd-aligned name/count columns + status badge; (2) the top summary line (renderHumanReport first writeStdout) → sectionBar("fabric doctor · <target>") + trailing status badge; (3) renderTldrHeader 'TL;DR' block → sectionBar("Checks") + tree of top issues; (4) issue section titles (writeIssueSection) → sectionBar. Import sectionBar/scopeBadge from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/theme, tree/grid from ../tui/structure.js, paint/symbol from ../colors.js. Refactor the rendering bodies into exported pure fns (renderDoctorHeader(report):string, renderDoctorStoreHealth(diagnostics):string, renderDoctorChecks(report):string) that return strings; the writeStdout wrappers call them. Leave the args.json===true branch untouched.

criteria: doctor.ts imports sectionBar from @fenglimg/fabric-shared/theme and tree/grid from ../tui/structure.js (grep both)
the literal `paint.ai("store health")` no longer appears in doctor.ts (grep -c returns 0); replaced by sectionBar("Store Health")
doctor store-health + checks render via tree() and/or grid() (grep -E 'tree\(|grid\(' packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts returns >=1)
the args.json===true output path is unchanged (git diff shows no edit inside the `if (args.json === true)` blocks)
a NO_COLOR snapshot test for the pure doctor render helpers exists and passes (toMatchSnapshot, NO_COLOR=1)
pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test exits 0 (existing doctor render tests + new snapshot)
convergence.verification: pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-cli test -- doctor && pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit
convergence.definition_of_done: fabric doctor renders section-bar + tree + grid + badges per mockup #1; JSON output is untouched; the new look is snapshot-pinned for NO_COLOR.

files.change: Replace flat coloured lines with sectionBar headers + tree/grid rows + status/scope badges; extract pure render* string helpers; leave JSON path intact.
Snapshot renderDoctorHeader/renderDoctorStoreHealth/renderDoctorChecks over fixture DoctorReport + StoreDiagnostic[] under NO_COLOR=1.

implementation: Map mockup #1 to the existing render fns: header line, store-health block, TL;DR/checks block, issue sections.
Extract each block's string composition into an exported pure fn taking the already-computed report/diagnostics data.
Rewrite bodies using sectionBar + tree (store rows, last row →└─) + grid padEnd alignment (name | count columns) + scopeBadge + status badge.
Keep writeStdout wrappers calling the pure fns; do not touch the JSON branch.
Add doctor-reskin.test.ts with fixture data + NO_COLOR snapshot.
Run doctor tests + tsc; commit (incremental per-surface).

reference.pattern: existing exported renderTldrHeader as a pure-fn precedent in doctor.ts; establish a NEW vitest toMatchSnapshot test with NO_COLOR=1 (grouped-help.test.ts uses toContain/toMatch, not toMatchSnapshot, and does not pin NO_COLOR — do not reuse it; add a fresh NO_COLOR=1 snapshot test)

rationale.chosen_approach: Reskin only the human path and extract pure render fns so the new look is snapshot-testable; JSON contract (CI/dashboards) is sacred and left byte-identical.
decision_factors: JSON output is a stable downstream contract
renderTldrHeader already exported as pure-ish fn — follow that
rationale.tradeoffs: doctor.ts is large (1650 lines) with many flags; only the default human report surface is reskinned — report sub-surfaces (cite-coverage/history) stay as-is to bound scope.

risks: doctor has many report sub-renderers; over-reaching risks churn — scope is the default report human path only.
Accidentally editing the JSON branch — guarded by a git-diff convergence criterion.
